steps.title: Identify the angle correspondence under dilation
steps.content: When a figure is dilated, all side lengths scale by the scale factor, but angles are preserved—they remain unchanged. Triangle $ABC$ is dilated by scale factor \$3$ to produce triangle $XYZ$, so corresponding vertices are: $A \leftrightarrow X$, $B \leftrightarrow Y$, and $C \leftrightarrow Z$. Therefore, angle $X$ (at vertex $X$ in the dilated triangle) equals angle $A$ (at the corresponding vertex $A$ in the original triangle).

steps.title: Read angle A from the diagram and conclude
steps.content: From the diagram, angle $A$ in triangle $ABC$ is labeled as \$60°$. Since dilation preserves angles, angle $X = $ angle $A = 60°$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
